item.title: Elizabeth Cosplay (Bioshock Infinite) by LittleLuckyCosplay
item.description:
I usually decide to cosplay a character by doing a makeup test first and seeing if the likeness is good enough (apologies for the Instagram text and sticker, I pulled this from my stories archives and didn’t have it saved anywhere else)
I also tend to favor cosplays that are weathered or lived in because it helps take away from that squeaky clean costume feel
I 3D printed both cameos to have options and painted with simple acrylics/sealed with a gloss coat. Went with the cage only because the gold matched the outfit better (in game it’s bird 100%)
Plus some bonus memes bc that’s my fave part of cosplay
item.dc:creator: LittleLuckyCosplay

item.title: Always has to be the center of attention.
item.dc:creator: countervail247365

item.title: Feminist icon INSISTS on public trial
item.description:
(All quotes from New York Times, sauce below) Feminist activists and writers have penned open letters to her that have been published in newspapers and read on the radio. They laud her courage, her strength, her dignity in confronting her horrifying story. They also praise her rare decision to fling open the doors onto her intimate hell and to insist that the trial be made public, when it could have stayed behind closed doors. Many victims feel she speaks for them. As Hélène Devynck, a journalist and author, wrote in the newspaper Le Monde: “It is not just you, Gisèle, that they treated like a thing. They tell us all, we are insignificant. Your strength gives us back ours. Thank you for this immense gift.”
Ms. Pelicot is at the center of the most significant rape trial France has seen in decades. Her husband of 50 years, Dominique Pelicot, has pleaded guilty to putting drugs in her food and drinks for almost a decade. Then he invited men into their bedroom to join him in raping her while she was drugged. Mr. Pelicot and most of the other men on trial are charged with the aggravated rape of Ms. Pelicot. More than a dozen of the men have pleaded guilty. Most of the rest do not dispute that they had sex with Ms. Pelicot, but they say that they did not think it was rape. Instead, they say they were tricked into it, lured by her husband for playful three-way encounters, and told that she was pretending to be asleep or some variation of that. Before her husband’s arrest, Ms. Pelicot, 71, led a quiet life: a retired manager at a big company, a mother of three and a grandmother of seven who had moved with her husband of 50 years to a small town in Provence to enjoy hiking in the hills and swimming in the backyard pool. Now, she arrives at court each day, dressed impeccably for battle, embodying the phrase her lawyers coined at the beginning of the trial that has become a mantra among her supporters — that shame must change sides, from the victim to the accused.
“Her head held high, she sweeps past the defendants who fill the room’s many benches. They range in age from 26 to 74. They are thin, fat, bearded, smooth-faced. Many are married and have children. They work as truck drivers, construction workers, tradesmen, salesmen. There is a journalist, a nurse, a prison guard and a tech specialist among them. By opening the doors to the public, Ms. Pelicot has opened up the view not only onto her own collapsing life and the legal process around rape but also onto the regular, mundane, normal profiles of the accused men. And many women credit her with skewering the myth of the monster rapist. “Friend of the family, stranger at a bar or the street, brother or cousin, friend, colleague, professor, neighbor: All women can sadly find a face that brings them back to a traumatic memory among the multitudes of accused,” said an open letter published in the French daily Libération that was signed by more than 260 artists, writers, politicians, activists and historians — mostly women. ——————— Very brave of her to insist that those awful videos be played in the court for all to see. It makes plain the situation for women and demands that society witness its own ugliness. And while defense lawyers try to say she asked for it, is an exhibitionist, or that her husbands’ consent overrules hers… she’s maintaining her composure & taking French culture to court with her. What a badass. ——————— Sauce: https://www.nytimes.com/2024/09/25/world/europe/france-rape-trial-gisele-pelicot.html?smid=nytcore-ios-share&referringSource=articleShare&sgrp=c-cb
item.dc:creator: TearEmUpTara

Thanks for scrolling. Have a nice day.

item.title: Phone Clearing Dump Pt 2 The Dumpening
item.description:
Read a history book
I'm multiple of these, send help
I could watch kids falling off bikes all day
I am 30 or 40 years old and I do not have time for this
Been playing Space Marine 2 and Darktide lately and I can't decide which is more grimdark
Yeah sorry I slipped a serious one in
Try a tabletop game, it doesn't have to be DnD, there are so many
I hate anxiety
Bonk!
I feel you Daffy, I feel you
Dog Tax. This is Fiona she is 13 years old and still acts like a puppy when going for walks.
item.dc:creator: Gheldan

item.title: Welp, it got vandalized. Time to put up another one.
item.description: Our flag got ripped off our house, pole included. Gonna need a new pole. But we're gonna get another flag and put that one up too. Maybe even add a second one. https://imgur.com/gallery/flag-up-hands-shaking-eL1QHoO

item.title: Misirlou (Dick Dale)
item.description:
https://www.youtube.com/shorts/k37zWR2ykm8 Now that I have your attention
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t-GtC-pHUFo (1963) Music owes so much to this man. Dick Dale and Leo Fender, Freddy Tavares are the pioneers of Fender equipment in the 1950s. Leo Fender was like a second father to Dick as when they met in the 1950s they collaborated on Loud Sound and much of the Fender Equipment such as Amps, Speakers, Transformers as well as Guitars, Strat Guitar, and more
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lRH_70_Foow (1996) He killed it for so long.
Dagoth Ur: "Come to me openly, and not by stealth." Nerevarine: "Vaya Con Dios."
item.dc:creator: BeastofKnowledge

item.title: Anyone Seen Tyler??
item.description: Licensor of video: "We went to our neighborhoods movie in the park and suddenly the speaker asked if anyone was missing a kid because there was a kid at the top of the tree. After a few minutes the kid climbed down and continued playing as usual." He climbed back down safely. Location: Seattle August 1924 Edit: 2024 lol...my bad

item.title: Year: Sculpture - Artist
item.description: •Year: Sculpture - Artist • 1411: Saint John the Evangelist - Donatello • 1499: Pietà - Michelangelo • 1515: Moses - Michelangelo • 1599: Hercules and the Centaur Nessus - Giambologna • 1624: David - Gian Lorenzo Bernini • 1752: Modesty - Antonio Corradini • 1801: Perseus with the Head of Medusa - Antonio Canova • 1850: The Veiled Virgin - Giovanni Strazza • 1880: Undine Rising from the Waters - Chauncey Ives
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007

item.title: My silly art.
item.description: This is one of my favorite paintings. I'd like to write a story about it one day. I'll try to make the time... although I feel like there's never enough time nowadays lol. Maybe it's because I'm 35 lol. Have a good night! I'm going to work the breakfast shift tomorrow it's going to be crazy lol

item.title: Images of Note: a wee fire?
item.description:
Americans will never understand
mood
Hey Abbie, what's it like living in Scotland?
FUCK!
:)
Egg breaker
BALL REMOVAL
Can we just talk about how a Former President is making money from Sales of Bibles that contain government documents as part of them *and* that Oklahoma is going to be buying them as part of compulsory Bible classes?
Yeah *drinks whisky*
Why the fuck is the US offering University places for Sports Prowess?
I DIDN'T *sobs*
http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging
item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

item.title: I've lost both of my bb kitties within a month
item.description:
I had to put Athena to sleep due to multiple health complications. At 16 years old, she'd had a good run and it was her time to go. (Sorry for the selfie)
Kimchi died unexpectedly a month later for unknown reasons. She was only 3 years old. She was found on our neighbor's porch, bleeding from her mouth. She died almost immediately after she was found. Losing both of my babies sucks. Hold your furry bb's tight. You never know when you might lose them. *Edit I thank you for the kind words. Distract me with your favorite dad joke!
item.dc:creator: AlcoholicSwampWitch
